# Index is auto-generated — never hand-edit

**Changed 2026-07-20.** `output/html/index.html` (the research homepage) and
`output/html/portfolio.html` are now generated by scripts, not edited by hand.
The old rule — add a `<tr class="stock-row">` to `index.html` for every new
research file — is retired. Those hand-added rows would be overwritten on the
next rebuild.

## What to do when you create or update a research file

1. Write `research/SYMBOL.md` with the standard header so the generator can read it.
   The parser (`scripts/build_site_index.py`) pulls, from the top of the file:
   - **Verdict** — a `**Verdict: …**` or `**Status:** …` line. This drives the
     filter bucket (Buy / Buy-at / Hold / Watchlist / Trim / Exit / Avoid). If it
     is missing, the note still appears, bucketed "Unclassified" — it is never dropped.
   - **Grade** — a `Grade B+` token or an `N/25` score.
   - **Date** — a `**Date:**` or `**Last Updated:**` line.
   Exchange tickers have no underscore; underscore-named files (`MACRO_NOTES`,
   `GROWW_vs_ICICIAMC`, screens) are auto-routed to the "Notes" bucket.

2. Regenerate the pages (also run every morning by the daily cron):
   ```
   venv/bin/python3 scripts/build_site_index.py       # research index (fresh CMP)
   venv/bin/python3 scripts/build_portfolio_page.py   # portfolio page (fresh CMP)
   ```

3. Commit the regenerated `index.html` / `portfolio.html` with your research change.

## Do NOT

- Hand-edit `index.html` or `portfolio.html` — changes are lost on rebuild.
- Run `src/refresh_portfolio.py --write` to update the site — it is retired for
  HTML output (now a read-only broker-reconciliation reporter). Prices there
  would be stale broker-export closes, not the fresh bhavcopy.
- Prices shown are the official bhavcopy close (end-of-day), not live. CMP is
  blank for names with no matched exchange ticker (US, some BSE-SME) — that is
  correct, not a bug; never fill it with a guessed number.

The index is still the GitHub Pages homepage — but it now reflects
`research/*.md` automatically. If a note isn't showing, check its header parses,
not the index HTML.
